title: Pension Credit
summary: Pension Credit is extra money to help with your living costs if you are over State Pension age and on a low income.

rules.plain: This benefit tops up your income if you're over 66 and living on a low income.
You need to live in England, Scotland or Wales and have reached State Pension age — that's 66 or over.
If you're on your own, you can usually claim when your weekly income is below £218. With a partner it's below £333.
Around 800,000 households who could get this never claim it. On average that's over £3,900 a year going unclaimed.
